{{< include /_macros.qmd >}}

# Preface {.unnumbered}

<!--
The preface sells the book and sets the contract with the reader.
Keep it short. Suggested beats below — write them in your own voice.
-->

## Why this book {.unnumbered}

<!-- What gap does this fill? "A compact, first-principles account of
     pretraining — the math-textbook companion to a nanoGPT-style repo." -->

## What this book is not {.unnumbered}

<!-- Scope guardrails: pretraining only (no post-training/RLHF for now);
     modern viewpoint, not a history of RNN -> attention. -->

## How to read it {.unnumbered}

<!-- The dependency graph between parts; how code and math interleave;
     what to skip on a first pass. -->

## Prerequisites {.unnumbered}

<!-- Point at Appendix B. Linear algebra, probability, autodiff, some PyTorch. -->

## The companion code {.unnumbered}

<!-- The `thebook` package: static listings in the text, runnable in src/.
     One paragraph on how to run it (uv sync). See Appendix C. -->

## Acknowledgements {.unnumbered}

<!-- TODO -->
